original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. On the view menu click large icons. On the view menu click small icons.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. On the view menu click large icons. On the view menu click small icons.

original_text: Click start point to settings click control panel and then double-click regional options. Under language settings for the system click to select the japanese check box. In the your locale (location) list click japanese and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings, click control panel, and then double-click regional options. Under language settings for the system, click to select the Japanese check box. In the your locale (location) list, click Japanese and then click OK.

original_text: Click start point to settings click control panel and the double-click system. On the hardware tab click device manager.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings, click control panel, and then double-click system. On the hardware tab, click device manager.

original_text: Click start point to settings click control panel and then double-click display. Click the screen saver tab click the channel screen saver in the screen saver box and then click settings. Click to select the desired channel screen savers in the channels box click ok and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings, click control panel, and then double-click display. Click the screen saver tab, click the channel screen saver in the screen saver box, and then click settings. Click to select the desired channel screen savers in the channels box, click ok, and then click ok.

original_text: Right-Click the internet explorer icon on your desktop and then click properties. On the security tab click the internet icon and then click custom level. Change the run activex controls and plug-ins option to either enable or prompt. Click ok.
resolved_text: Right-Click the internet explorer icon on your desktop and then click properties. On the security tab click the internet explorer icon and then click custom level. Change the run ActiveX controls and plug-ins option to either enable or prompt. Click ok.

original_text: Start internet explorer. On the tools menu click internet options. Click the advanced tab. Click to clear or select the enable personalized favorites menu check box. Click ok.
resolved_text: Start Internet Explorer. On the tools menu click Internet options. Click the advanced tab. Click to clear or select the enable personalized favorites menu check box. Click OK.

original_text: Right-Click the folder and then click properties. On the general tab click advanced. Click to select the for fast searching allow the index service to index this folder check box click ok and then click apply. When you are prompted to apply these settings click all folders sub-folders and files and then click ok.
resolved_text: Right-Click the folder and then click properties. On the general tab click advanced. Click to select the "for fast searching allow the index service to index this folder" check box, click ok, and then click apply. When you are prompted to apply these settings, click all folders, sub-folders, and files, and then click ok.

original_text: Click start click run in the open box type dcomcnfg and then click ok. On the default properties tab click any setting other than anonymous in the default impersonation level box the default setting is identify.
resolved_text: Click start, click run, in the open box type dcomcnfg, and then click ok. On the default properties tab, click any setting other than anonymous in the default impersonation level box; the default setting is identify.

original_text: Start windows explorer. On the tools menu click folder options. Click the file types tab. In the registered file types box click the htm extension and then click advanced. In the actions box click print and then click edit. Replace the entry in the application used to perform action box with the following line type it as shown rundll32.exe c:\winnt\system32\mshtml.dll printhtml "%1". Click ok click ok and then click ok.
resolved_text: Start Windows Explorer. On the tools menu click folder options. Click the file types tab. In the registered file types box click the htm extension and then click advanced. In the actions box click print and then click edit. Replace the entry in the application used to perform action box with the following line type the entry as shown rundll32.exe c:\winnt\system32\mshtml.dll printhtml "%1". Click ok click ok and then click ok.

original_text: Click start point to settings click control panel and then double-click power options. Click the ups tab and then click select. In the on port box click your com port. Click finish and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings, click control panel, and then double-click power options. Click the ups tab and then click select. In the on port box, click your com port. Click finish and then click ok.

original_text: Start internet explorer. On the view menu click toolbars and then click customize. In the customize toolbar dialog box click the down arrow in the icon options box click large icons and then click close.
resolved_text: Start Internet Explorer. On the view menu click toolbars and then click customize. In the customize toolbar dialog box click the down arrow in the icon options box, click large icons, and then click close.

original_text: Click start click run and then type dcomcnfg in the open box. Click ok and click the default properties tab. Click identify in the default impersonation level box. Click apply or ok.
resolved_text: Click start, click run, and then type dcomcnfg in the open box. Click ok and click the default properties tab. Click identify in the default impersonation level box. Click apply or ok.

original_text: Start internet explorer. On the tools menu click internet options. On the general tab click accessibility. Click to select the ignore font sizes specified on web pages check box. Click ok twice.
resolved_text: Start Internet Explorer. On the tools menu click Internet options. On the general tab click accessibility. Click to select the ignore font sizes specified on web pages check box. Click OK twice.

original_text: Double-Click my computer. On the tools menu click folder options. Click the file types tab. Under registered file types click to select doc microsoft word document or xls microsoft excel worksheet and then click advanced. Click to clear the confirm open after download check box and then click ok.
resolved_text: Double-Click my computer. On the tools menu click folder options. Click the file types tab. Under registered file types click to select doc microsoft word document or xls microsoft excel worksheet and then click advanced. Click to clear the confirm open after download check box and then click ok.

original_text: Right-Click my computer and then click manage. Double-Click system tools. Double-Click local users and groups. Double-Click users. On the action menu click new user.
resolved_text: Right-Click my computer and then click manage. Double-Click system tools. Double-Click local users and groups. Double-Click users. On the action menu click new user.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click keyboard click the input locales tab and then click add. In the input locale box click basque. In the keyboard layout/ime box click spanish variation. Click ok and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click keyboard, click the input locales tab and then click add. In the input locale box, click basque. In the keyboard layout/ime box, click spanish variation. Click ok and then click ok.

original_text: Click start point to programs point to accessories point to accessability and then click magnifier. Click the follow mouse cursor check box to select it and then click exit.
resolved_text: Click start point to programs point to accessories point to accessibility and then click magnifier. Click the follow mouse cursor check box to select the follow mouse cursor check box and then click exit.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click power options. On the advanced tab click to select the prompt for password when computer goes off standby check box. Click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click power options. On the advanced tab click to select the prompt for password when the computer goes off standby check box. Click ok.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click users and passwords. On the advanced tab click advanced.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click users and passwords. On the advanced tab click advanced.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click users and passwords. Click to select the users must enter a user name and password to use this computer check box.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click users and passwords. Click to select the users must enter a user name and password to use the computer check box.

original_text: Click start and then click run. In the open box type conf.exe and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start and then click run. In the open box type conf.exe and then click ok.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click system. On the advanced tab click startup and recovery. Click to clear the automatically reboot check box and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click system. On the advanced tab click startup and recovery. Click to clear the automatically reboot check box and then click ok.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click accessibility options. Under filterkeys click settings. Under keyboard shortcut click to clear the use shortcut check box.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click accessibility options. Under filterkeys click settings. Under keyboard shortcut click to clear the use shortcut check box.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click display. Click the effects tab. Click to clear the show window contents while dragging check box. Click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click display. Click the effects tab. Click to clear the show window contents while dragging check box. Click ok.

original_text: On the start menu click help. Click the search tab. In the type in the keyword to find box type large fonts. Click list topics.
resolved_text: On the start menu click help. Click the search tab. In the type in the keyword to find box type large fonts. Click list topics.

original_text: Double-Click my computer and then right-click local disk (c:). Click properties and then click tools. In error-checking click check now.
resolved_text: Double-Click my computer and then right-click local disk (c:). Click properties and then click tools. In error-checking, click check now.

original_text: Click start point to programs point to administrative tools and then click local security policy. In the left pane click local policies and then in the right pane double-click security options. In the right pane double-click secure channel: digitally encrypt or sign secure channel data (always). In the local security policy setting dialog box click disabled and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to programs point to administrative tools and then click local security policy. In the left pane click local policies and then in the right pane double-click security options. In the right pane double-click secure channel: digitally encrypt or sign secure channel data (always). In the local security policy setting dialog box click disabled and then click OK.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click administrative tools and then double-click event viewer. Right-Click the system log file and then click properties. Click overwrite as needed or set a larger maximum log file size. Click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-click administrative tools and then double-click event viewer. Right-click the system log file and then click properties. Click overwrite as needed or set a larger maximum system log file size. Click ok.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click system. On the hardware tab click device manager. In device manager click the plus symbol next to dvd/cd-rom drives and then click your cdrw/dvd drive. On the action menu click properties. On the properties tab click to select the enable digital cd audio for this cd-rom device. Click ok and then restart your computer when you are prompted.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click system. On the hardware tab click device manager. In device manager click the plus symbol next to dvd/cd-rom drives and then click your cdrw/dvd drive. On the action menu click properties. On the properties tab click to select the enable digital cd audio for the cd-rom device. Click ok and then restart the computer when you are prompted.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click network and dial-up connections. Right-Click the dial-up adapter and then click properties. Click the networking tab. In the type of dial-up server i am calling box click ppp: windows 95/98/nt/2000 internet. Click settings. Click to clear the negotiate multi-link for single link connections check box. Click ok and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click network and dial-up connections. Right-Click the dial-up adapter and then click properties. Click the networking tab. In the type of dial-up server the user is calling box click ppp: windows 95/98/nt/2000 internet. Click settings. Click to clear the negotiate multi-link for single link connections check box. Click ok and then click ok.

original_text: Click start point to programs click administrative tools and then double-click local security policy. Double-Click local policies. Double-Click security options. In the right pane double-click prevent users from installing printer drivers. Click disabled and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to programs, click administrative tools, and then double-click local security policy. Double-click local policies. Double-click security options. In the right pane, double-click prevent users from installing printer drivers. Click disabled and then click OK.

original_text: Click start click run in the open box type secpol.msc and then click ok. Double-Click local policies. Double-Click user rights assignment. Double-Click back up files and directories. Click add and then double-click the administrators group. Click ok.
resolved_text: Click start, click run, in the open box type secpol.msc, and then click ok. Double-Click local policies. Double-Click user rights assignment. Double-Click back up files and directories. Click add and then double-click the administrators group. Click ok.

original_text: Click start point to programs point to administrative tools and then click local security policy. Under security settings double-click local policies and then click security options. Double-Click additional restrictions for anonymous connections and then under local policy setting click not defined or none. rely on default permissions.
resolved_text: Click start point to programs point to administrative tools and then click local security policy. Under security settings double-click local policies and then click security options. Double-click additional restrictions for anonymous connections and then under local policy setting click not defined or none. Rely on default permissions.

original_text: Right-Click start and then click explore. On the tools menu click folder options. Click the view tab and then in the advanced settings list under files and folders do the following. Under hidden files and folders click show hidden files and folders. Click to clear the hide file extensions for known file types check box.
resolved_text: Right-Click start and then click explore. On the tools menu click folder options. Click the view tab and then in the advanced settings list under files and folders do the following. Under hidden files and folders click show hidden files and folders. Click to clear the hide file extensions for known file types check box.

original_text: Click start and then click help. On the search tab type internet connection sharing in the type in the keyword to find box and then click list topics. Double-Click internet connection sharing in the select topic box.
resolved_text: Click start and then click help. On the search tab type internet connection sharing in the type in the keyword to find box and then click list topics. Double-Click internet connection sharing in the select topic box.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click network and dial-up connections. Right-Click the appropriate dial-up network and then click properties. Click the networking tab and then click settings. Click to clear the negotiate multi-link for single link connections check box if it is selected. Click ok and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click network and dial-up connections. Right-Click the appropriate dial-up network and then click properties. Click the networking tab and then click settings. Click to clear the negotiate multi-link for single link connections check box if the check box is selected. Click ok and then click ok.

original_text: Click start click run type services.msc in the open box and then click ok. In the list of services double-click tcp/ip netbios helper service. In the startup type list click automatic and then click apply. Under service status click start to start the tcp/ip netbios helper service. When the service is started click ok and then quit the services snap-in.
resolved_text: Click start, click run, type services.msc in the open box, and then click ok. In the list of services, double-click tcp/ip netbios helper service. In the startup type list, click automatic and then click apply. Under service status, click start to start the tcp/ip netbios helper service. When the tcp/ip netbios helper service is started, click ok and then quit the services snap-in.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click printers. Click the paintjet printer and then click properties on the file menu. Click the device settings tab. Click send ctrl-d after each job and then click no in the list box.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click printers. Click the paintjet printer and then click properties on the file menu. Click the device settings tab. Click send ctrl-d after each job and then click no in the list box.

original_text: Right-Click my network places and then click properties. Right-Click the local area connection icon and then click properties. Click install click client and then click add. In the network client box click client for microsoft networks and then click ok. In the local area connections properties window click ok.
resolved_text: Right-Click my network places and then click properties. Right-Click the local area connection icon and then click properties. Click install, click client, and then click add. In the network client box, click client for Microsoft networks and then click ok. In the local area connections properties window, click ok.

original_text: Click start and then point to settings. Click network and dial-up connections. Double-Click the applicable local area connection and then click properties. In the components pane click internet protocol (tcp/ip) and then click properties. Click advanced click the wins tab and then click enable netbios over tcp/ip. Click ok to close all the dialog boxes.
resolved_text: Click start and then point to settings. Click network and dial-up connections. Double-click the applicable local area connection and then click properties. In the components pane, click internet protocol (tcp/ip) and then click properties. Click advanced, click the wins tab, and then click enable netbios over tcp/ip. Click ok to close all the dialog boxes.

original_text: Click start point to programs point to administrative tools and then click services. Right-Click internet connection sharing and then click properties. Under startup type click disabled. Under service status click stop. Click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to programs point to administrative tools and then click services. Right-Click internet connection sharing and then click properties. Under startup type click disabled. Under service status click stop. Click ok.

original_text: Start windows explorer. On the tools menu click folder options. Click the view tab. Click to clear the launch folder windows in a separate process check box and then click ok.
resolved_text: Start Windows Explorer. On the tools menu click folder options. Click the view tab. Click to clear the launch folder windows in a separate process check box and then click OK.

original_text: Right-Click the my computer icon on your desktop and then click manage. In the computer management console double-click services and applications and then click services. In the rightmost window right-click dhcp client and then click properties. Change startup type to automatic and then click ok. Right-Click the dhcp client service again and then click start.
resolved_text: Right-Click the my computer icon on your desktop and then click manage. In the computer management console double-click services and applications and then click services. In the rightmost window right-click the dhcp client and then click properties. Change the startup type to automatic and then click ok. Right-Click the dhcp client service again and then click start.

original_text: Click start point to settings click control panel and then double-click network and dial-up connections. Right-Click local area connection and then click properties. Click install click protocol and then click add. Click network monitor driver and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings, click control panel, and then double-click network and dial-up connections. Right-click local area connection and then click properties. Click install, click protocol, and then click add. Click network monitor driver and then click ok.

original_text: Right-Click my network places and then click properties. Double-Click local area connection and then click properties. Click to select the file and print sharing for microsoft networks check box and then click ok.
resolved_text: Right-Click my network places and then click properties. Double-Click local area connection and then click properties. Click to select the file and print sharing for Microsoft networks check box and then click ok.

original_text: Click start point to programs point to administrative tools and then click services. Right-Click internet connection sharing and then click start.
resolved_text: Click start point to programs point to administrative tools and then click services. Right-Click internet connection sharing and then click start.

original_text: Click start point to settings and the click printers. In the printers dialog box right-click your printer and then click properties. On the advanced tab click print processor. In the default datatype list click raw and then click ok. Click ok to close the printer properties dialog box.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click printers. In the printers dialog box, right-click your printer and then click properties. On the advanced tab, click print processor. In the default datatype list, click raw and then click ok. Click ok to close the printer properties dialog box.

original_text: Click start point to settings click control panel and then double-click network and dial-up connections. Right-Click local area connection and then click properties. Click install click service in the select network component type box and then click add. Click sap agent and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings, click control panel, and then double-click network and dial-up connections. Right-click local area connection and then click properties. Click install, click service in the select network component type box, and then click add. Click sap agent and then click ok.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click printers. Right-Click the hp 1600 cm/ps printer and then click properties. On the advanced tab click new driver. Click next. In the manufacturers box click hp in the models box click 1600 cm/ps. Click next or finish until the wizard finishes.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click printers. Right-Click the hp 1600 cm/ps printer and then click properties. On the advanced tab click new driver. Click next. In the manufacturers box click hp in the models box click 1600 cm/ps. Click next or finish until the wizard finishes.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click network and dial-up connection. Right-Click local area connection and then click properties. Click internet protocol (tcp/ip) and then click properties. Click advanced and then click the wins tab. Click enable netbios over tcp/ip. Click ok you can disregard the empty primary wins address notification. Click ok and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click network and dial-up connection. Right-Click local area connection and then click properties. Click internet protocol (tcp/ip) and then click properties. Click advanced and then click the wins tab. Click enable netbios over tcp/ip. Click ok; you can disregard the empty primary wins address notification. Click ok and then click ok.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click network and dial-up connections. Click local area connection and then click properties on the file menu click install. Click clients click add click client for microsoft networks and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click network and dial-up connections. Click local area connection and then click properties on the file menu, click install. Click clients, click add, click client for Microsoft networks, and then click ok.

original_text: Click tools and then choose internet options. Click the advanced tab. Click to select the show pictures check box. Click ok.
resolved_text: Click tools and then choose internet options. Click the advanced tab. Click to select the show pictures check box. Click OK.

original_text: On the tools menu click internet options and then click the advanced tab. In the settings box under printing click to select the print background colors and images check box and then click ok.
resolved_text: On the tools menu click internet options and then click the advanced tab. In the settings box under printing click to select the print background colors and images check box and then click OK.

original_text: Click internet options on the tools menu and then click the security tab. Click internet in the select a web content zone to specify its security settings section and then click custom level. In the run activex scripts and plugins section click disable and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click internet options on the tools menu and then click the security tab. Click internet in the select a web content zone to specify the security settings section and then click custom level. In the run ActiveX scripts and plugins section click disable and then click ok.

original_text: On the tools menu in internet explorer click internet options. Click the advanced tab. Click to clear the do not save encrypted pages to disk check box and then click ok.
resolved_text: On the tools menu in Internet Explorer, click Internet Options. Click the advanced tab. Click to clear the "do not save encrypted pages to disk" check box and then click OK.

original_text: On the tools menu in internet explorer click internet options. On the general tab under temporary internet files click delete files and then click ok on the delete files dialog box. Click ok to close the internet options dialog box. Click the refresh button at the top of the browser to ensure that you are viewing the latest version of the current page.
resolved_text: On the tools menu in Internet Explorer, click Internet Options. On the General tab under Temporary Internet Files, click Delete Files and then click OK on the Delete Files dialog box. Click OK to close the Internet Options dialog box. Click the Refresh button at the top of the browser to ensure that you are viewing the latest version of the current page.

original_text: In internet explorer click internet options on the tools menu. Click the advanced tab. Click to select the show internet explorer on desktop check box.
resolved_text: In Internet Explorer, click Internet Options on the Tools menu. Click the Advanced tab. Click to select the Show Internet Explorer on Desktop check box.

original_text: In internet explorer click internet options on the tools menu. On the general tab click languages. Click english [united states][en-us] and then click ok. Click ok to close the language preference dialog box click ok to close the internet options dialog box.
resolved_text: In Internet Explorer, click Internet Options on the Tools menu. On the General tab, click Languages. Click English [United States][en-US] and then click OK. Click OK to close the Language Preference dialog box, and click OK to close the Internet Options dialog box.

original_text: In internet explorer click internet options on the tools menu. On the privacy tab click advanced. Click to select the override automatic cookie handling check box. Click prompt for both first-party cookies and third-party cookies. Click ok twice.
resolved_text: In Internet Explorer, click Internet Options on the Tools menu. On the Privacy tab, click Advanced. Click to select the Override Automatic Cookie Handling check box. Click Prompt for both first-party cookies and third-party cookies. Click OK twice.

original_text: In control panel double-click display. Click none and then click ok.
resolved_text: In control panel, double-click the display. Click none and then click ok.

original_text: Open control panel and then click power options. Click the power schemes tab. Under power schemes click always on.
resolved_text: Open control panel and then click power options. Click the power schemes tab. Under power schemes, click always on.

original_text: Double-Click my computer. On the tools menu click folder options. Click the offline files tab. Click to select the enable offline files check box click apply and then click ok.
resolved_text: Double-Click my computer. On the tools menu click folder options. Click the offline files tab. Click to select the enable offline files check box, click apply, and then click ok.

original_text: On the start menu point to programs point to administrative tools and then click services. Click routing and remote access and then click the stop symbol. Click remote registry service and then click the start symbol. Click routing and remote access and then click the start symbol.
resolved_text: On the start menu, point to programs, point to administrative tools, and then click services. Click routing and remote access and then click the stop symbol. Click remote registry service and then click the start symbol. Click routing and remote access and then click the start symbol.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click system. On the network identification tab click network id. In the network identification wizard click next. Click this computer is for home use and not part of a business network and click next. Click users must enter a user name and password. Click next and then click finish.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click system. On the network identification tab click network id. In the network identification wizard click next. Click the option that states this computer is for home use and not part of a business network and click next. Click the option that states users must enter a user name and password. Click next and then click finish.

original_text: Double-Click my computer and then double-click dial-up networking. Right-Click the appropriate dial-up network and then click properties. Click configure and then click to clear the enable modem error control check box. Click ok and then click ok.
resolved_text: Double-Click my computer and then double-click dial-up networking. Right-Click the appropriate dial-up network and then click properties. Click configure and then click to clear the enable modem error control check box. Click ok and then click ok.

original_text: Click start and then click control panel or point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click administrative tools. Double-Click services. Double-Click messenger. In the startup type list click disabled. Click stop and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start and then click control panel or point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click administrative tools. Double-Click services. Double-Click messenger. In the startup type list click disabled. Click stop and then click ok.

original_text: On the tools menu click folder options. On the view tab click to clear the hide protected operating system files check box.
resolved_text: On the tools menu click folder options. On the view tab click to clear the hide protected operating system files check box.

original_text: Double-Click my computer on the desktop. On the tools menu click folder options. On the offline files tab click view files. On the tools menu click synchronize. Click synchronize when you are prompted to synchronize.
resolved_text: Double-Click my computer on the desktop. On the tools menu click folder options. On the offline files tab click view files. On the tools menu click synchronize. Click synchronize when the system prompts to synchronize.

original_text: Click start point to programs point to administrative tools and then click services. Right-Click the print spooler service and then click start.
resolved_text: Click start point to programs point to administrative tools and then click services. Right-Click the print spooler service and then click start.

original_text: Click start point to settings click control panel and then double-click administrative tools. Double-Click component services click to select services (local) right-click print spooler and then click properties. Click start and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings, click control panel, and then double-click administrative tools. Double-click component services, click to select services (local), right-click print spooler, and then click properties. Click start and then click ok.

original_text: Start the magnifier tool. Click to check the follow mouse cursor check box and then minimize the magnifier tool.
resolved_text: Start the magnifier tool. Click to check the follow mouse cursor check box and then minimize the magnifier tool.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click network and dial-up connections. Click local area connection and then click properties on the file menu. Click install click service and then click add. Click file and printer sharing for microsoft networks and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click network and dial-up connections. Click local area connection and then click properties on the file menu. Click install, click service, and then click add. Click file and printer sharing for Microsoft networks and then click ok.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click sounds and multimedia. On the audio tab click advanced under sound playback. In the speaker setup box click the surround sound speakers you want to use and then click ok. Click ok and then close control panel.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click sounds and multimedia. On the audio tab click advanced under sound playback. In the speaker setup box click the surround sound speakers that you want to use and then click ok. Click ok and then close control panel.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click administrative tools then double-click event viewer. Click to expand event viewer if it is not already expanded. Click application.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-click administrative tools then double-click event viewer. Click to expand event viewer if the event viewer is not already expanded. Click application.

original_text: Right-Click my computer and then click manage. Double-Click system tools in the right pane and then double-click device manager. Double-Click ide ata/atapi controller in the right pane and then double-click the secondary ide channel. On the advanced settings tab click pio only in the transfer mode box. Click ok close the computer management window and then reboot the computer.
resolved_text: Right-Click my computer and then click manage. Double-Click system tools in the right pane and then double-click device manager. Double-Click ide ata/atapi controller in the right pane and then double-click the secondary ide channel. On the advanced settings tab click pio only in the transfer mode box. Click ok close the computer management window and then reboot the computer.

original_text: Click start point to programs point to accessories point to system tools and then click disk defragmenter. Click the disk that you want to defragment and then click defragment on the action menu.
resolved_text: Click start point to programs point to accessories point to system tools and then click disk defragmenter. Click the disk that the user wants to defragment and then click defragment on the action menu.

original_text: Click start point to settings click control panel and then double-click system. Click the hardware tab and then click device manager. Right-Click modems and then click scan for hardware changes.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings, click control panel, and then double-click system. Click the hardware tab and then click device manager. Right-click modems and then click scan for hardware changes.

original_text: On the desktop right-click my network places and then click properties. Right-Click the local area connection and then click properties. Click internet protocol (tcp/ip) and click properties. Click advanced and then click wins. If you are using a static internet protocol (ip) address click enable netbios over tcp/ip. Click ok in the open dialog boxes to close them and to save the new settings.
resolved_text: On the desktop, right-click my network places and then click properties. Right-click the local area connection and then click properties. Click internet protocol (tcp/ip) and click properties. Click advanced and then click wins. If you are using a static internet protocol (ip) address, click enable netbios over tcp/ip. Click ok in the open dialog boxes to close the dialog boxes and to save the new settings.

original_text: Right-Click my network places on the desktop and then click properties. Right-Click the icon for the local area connection and then click properties. On the networking tab click the internet protocol (tcp/ip) entry under components checked are used by this connection and then click properties. Click advanced. Click the dns tab. Click to clear the register this connection's address in dns check box. Click ok.
resolved_text: Right-Click my network places on the desktop and then click properties. Right-Click the icon for the local area connection and then click properties. On the networking tab click the internet protocol (tcp/ip) entry under components checked are used by the local area connection and then click properties. Click advanced. Click the dns tab. Click to clear the register this connection's address in dns check box. Click ok.

original_text: On the desktop right-click my network places and then click properties. Right-Click local area connection and then click properties. In the components checked are used by this connection list double-click internet protocol (tcp/ip) click advanced and then click the wins tab. Click use netbios setting from the dhcp server and then click ok three times.
resolved_text: On the desktop right-click my network places and then click properties. Right-click local area connection and then click properties. In the components checked are used by the local area connection list double-click internet protocol (tcp/ip), click advanced and then click the wins tab. Click use netbios setting from the dhcp server and then click ok three times.

original_text: Right-Click my computer and then click manage. Under system tools double-click local users and groups to expand it and then click users. In the right pane right-click the guest account and then click properties. Click to clear the account is disabled check box and make sure the password is set to null (blank) click ok.
resolved_text: Right-Click my computer and then click manage. Under system tools double-click local users and groups to expand the section and then click users. In the right pane right-click the guest account and then click properties. Click to clear the account is disabled check box and make sure the password is set to null (blank) click ok.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click taskbar & start menu. Click to set the show clock check box and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click taskbar & start menu. Click to set the show clock check box and then click the ok button.

original_text: Click start point to search and then click files or folders. In the files or folders named box type riched20.dll and then click search now. In the search results list right-click riched20.dll.
resolved_text: Click start point to search and then click files or folders. In the files or folders named box type riched20.dll and then click search now. In the search results list right-click the file riched20.dll.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click system. Click the advanced tab and then click settings under startup and recovery. In the write debugging information list click small memory dump (64k).
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click system. Click the advanced tab and then click settings under startup and recovery. In the write debugging information list click small memory dump (64k).

original_text: Click start point to programs point to administrative tools and performance and then click performance logs and alerts.
resolved_text: Click start point to programs point to administrative tools and performance and then click performance logs and alerts.

original_text: Right-Click my computer and then click manage. Open services and applications. Click services. Right-Click performance logs and alerts then click properties. Click the log on tab and then click this account.
resolved_text: Right-Click my computer and then click manage. Open services and applications. Click services. Right-Click performance logs and alerts then click properties. Click the log on tab and then click the account.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Click administrative tools. Double-Click services. Right-Click fax service and then click properties. Click the log on tab. Click this account.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Click administrative tools. Double-Click services. Right-Click fax service and then click properties. Click the log on tab. Click the account.

original_text: Right-Click my computer on the desktop and then click properties. On the advanced tab click startup and recovery. Change the value in the system startup to 10 seconds. Click ok and then click ok.
resolved_text: Right-Click my computer on the desktop and then click properties. On the advanced tab click startup and recovery. Change the value in the system startup to 10 seconds. Click ok and then click ok.

original_text: Click start right-click my computer and then click manage. In the console pane click device manager. On the view menu click show hidden devices. In the details pane expand network adapters. Right-Click direct parallel and then click disable.
resolved_text: Click start, right-click my computer, and then click manage. In the console pane, click device manager. On the view menu, click show hidden devices. In the details pane, expand network adapters. Right-click the direct parallel and then click disable.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click power options. Click the power schemes tab.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click power options. Click the power schemes tab.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click phone and modem options. Click the modems tab. Click your modem and then click properties. Click the diagnostics tab. Click query modem.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click phone and modem options. Click the modems tab. Click your modem and then click properties. Click the diagnostics tab. Click query modem.

original_text: Click start point to settings click control panel and then double-click system. Click the hardware tab and then click device manager. Double-Click the modems branch to expand it right-click 56000 bps v90 modem click properties and then click the modem tab. Change the maximum port speed to 115200 and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings, click control panel, and then double-click system. Click the hardware tab and then click device manager. Double-click the modems branch to expand it, right-click 56000 bps v90 modem, click properties, and then click the modem tab. Change the maximum port speed to 115200 and then click ok.

original_text: Click start and then click run. In the open box type regsvr32 /u inseng.dll and then click ok. When you receive the message that states that dllunregisterserver in inseng.dll succeeded click ok. Click start and then click run. In the open box type regsvr32 inseng.dll and then click ok. When you receive the message that states that dllregisterserver in inseng.dll succeeded click ok.
resolved_text: Click start and then click run. In the open box type regsvr32 /u inseng.dll and then click ok. When you receive the message that states that dllunregisterserver in inseng.dll succeeded click ok. Click start and then click run. In the open box type regsvr32 inseng.dll and then click ok. When you receive the message that states that dllregisterserver in inseng.dll succeeded click ok.

original_text: Click start point to settings point to control panel and then click display. Click the settings tab. Click the advanced button. Click the adapter tab. When you click the list all modes button a list of what functionality is inlcuded with this video card is displayed.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings point to control panel and then click display. Click the settings tab. Click the advanced button. Click the adapter tab. When you click the list all modes button, a list of what functionality is included with this video card is displayed.

original_text: Click start point to settings click control panel double-click administrative tools and then double-click computer management. If it is not already expanded expand the storage branch and then click the disk management folder to open it. On the action menu click restore basic disk configuration.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings, click control panel, double-click administrative tools, and then double-click computer management. If the computer management window is not already expanded, expand the storage branch and then click the disk management folder to open the disk management folder. On the action menu, click restore basic disk configuration.

original_text: Right-Click my network places and then click properties. In the network and dial-up connections window on the advanced menu click optional networking components. In the windows optional networking components wizard click to select the networking services check box and then click details. In the networking services dialog box click to select the rip listener check box click ok and then click next. Insert the operating system installation disc when you are prompted click ok and dns is reinstalled.
resolved_text: Right-Click my network places and then click properties. In the network and dial-up connections window on the advanced menu click optional networking components. In the Windows optional networking components wizard click to select the networking services check box and then click details. In the networking services dialog box click to select the rip listener check box, click ok, and then click next. Insert the operating system installation disc when the user is prompted, click ok, and DNS is reinstalled.

original_text: Right-Click my network places and then click properties. Right-Click the network adapter that is connected to the network that the computers running windows 95/windows 98 are using usually local area connection and then click properties. Click internet protocol (tcp/ip) and then click properties. Click the advanced button. On the wins tab click enable netbios over tcp/ip and then click ok. Click ok and then click ok.
resolved_text: Right-Click my network places and then click properties. Right-Click the network adapter that is connected to the network that the computers running Windows 95/Windows 98 are using, usually local area connection, and then click properties. Click Internet Protocol (TCP/IP) and then click properties. Click the advanced button. On the WINS tab, click enable NetBIOS over TCP/IP and then click OK. Click OK and then click OK.

original_text: Click start on the taskbar and then click run. Type the following in the open box tlntsvr /service. Start the telnet server service by clicking ok.
resolved_text: Click start on the taskbar and then click run. Type the following in the open box tlntsvr /service. Start the telnet server service by clicking ok.

original_text: Click start point to search and then click for files and folders. In the search for files and folders named box type msn.cms. Click search now.
resolved_text: Click start point to search and then click for files and folders. In the search for files and folders named box type msn.cms. Click search now.

original_text: Right-Click my network places and then click properties. Right-Click local area connection and then click properties. Click to select the file and printer sharing for microsoft networks check box. Click ok.
resolved_text: Right-Click my network places and then click properties. Right-Click local area connection and then click properties. Click to select the file and printer sharing for Microsoft networks check box. Click ok.

original_text: Click start point to programs point to administrative tools and then click computer management. Click device manager. Expand the modem branch right-click your modem and then click properties. Click the advanced tab. Click change default preferences. Configure the modem settings that you want to use click ok and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to programs point to administrative tools and then click computer management. Click device manager. Expand the modem branch, right-click your modem, and then click properties. Click the advanced tab. Click change default preferences. Configure the modem settings that you want to use, click ok, and then click ok.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click display. On the appearance tab click scrollbar in the item box. In the size box next to item decrease the size to a smaller value for example if it is set to 16 the default change it to 10. Click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click display. On the appearance tab click scrollbar in the item box. In the size box next to item decrease the size to a smaller value; for example, if the size is set to 16, the default, change the size to 10. Click ok.

original_text: Click start and then click run. In the open box type mmc and then click ok. On the console menu click add/remove snap-in and then click add. In the available standalone snap-ins list click security configuration and analysis click add and then click close. Click ok. In the console tree right-click security configuration and analysis and then click open database.
resolved_text: Click start and then click run. In the open box type mmc and then click ok. On the console menu click add/remove snap-in and then click add. In the available standalone snap-ins list click security configuration and analysis, click add and then click close. Click ok. In the console tree right-click security configuration and analysis and then click open database.

original_text: Click start click run type regedit in the open box and then click ok. In registry editor locate and then click the following subkey hkey_local_machine \ system \ currentcontrolset \ services \ tcpip \ parameters. In the right pane right-click enableicmpredirect or enableicmpredirects and then click modify. In the edit dword value dialog box type 1 in the value data box and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start, click run, type regedit in the open box, and then click ok. In the registry editor, locate and then click the following subkey hkey_local_machine \ system \ currentcontrolset \ services \ tcpip \ parameters. In the right pane, right-click enableicmpredirect or enableicmpredirects and then click modify. In the edit dword value dialog box, type 1 in the value data box and then click ok.

original_text: On the start menu click programs point to accessories point to communications and then click network and dial-up connections. Right-Click the dial-up icon and then click properties. In the network connection dialog box click the security tab. In security options click advanced (custom settings) and then click settings. In the advanced security settings dialog box in logon security click properties under use extensible authentication protocol (eap). In the smart card or other certificate properties dialog box click to clear the validate server certificate check box and then click ok.
resolved_text: On the start menu click programs point to accessories point to communications and then click network and dial-up connections. Right-Click the dial-up icon and then click properties. In the network connection dialog box click the security tab. In security options click advanced (custom settings) and then click settings. In the advanced security settings dialog box in logon security click properties under use extensible authentication protocol (EAP). In the smart card or other certificate properties dialog box click to clear the validate server certificate check box and then click OK.

original_text: Double-Click my computer. Double-Click the local disk (c:) drive. Right-Click the autoexec file and then click properties. Click the security tab. Click to clear the allow inheritable permissions from parent to propagate to this object check box. In the security dialog box that appears click remove. Click add. In the look in box click the name of the current computer. In the name box click system and then click add. Click ok. Click ok.
resolved_text: Double-Click my computer. Double-Click the local disk (c:) drive. Right-Click the autoexec file and then click properties. Click the security tab. Click to clear the allow inheritable permissions from parent to propagate to the autoexec file check box. In the security dialog box that appears click remove. Click add. In the look in box click the name of the current computer. In the name box click system and then click add. Click ok. Click ok.

original_text: From the start menu point to programs point to accessories and then click windows explorer. From the tools menu click folder options and go to the view tab. In the advanced settings area under the hidden files and folders select show hidden files and folders and then click ok. In the left pane of windows explorer expand my computer. Click local drive (c:). In the right pane find and select autoexec right-click this file. Click properties from the pop-up menu. On the general tab clear the hidden attribute and then click ok.
resolved_text: From the start menu point to programs point to accessories and then click windows explorer. From the tools menu click folder options and go to the view tab. In the advanced settings area under the hidden files and folders select show hidden files and folders and then click ok. In the left pane of windows explorer expand my computer. Click local drive (c:). In the right pane find and select autoexec right-click the file. Click properties from the pop-up menu. On the general tab clear the hidden attribute and then click ok.

original_text: Click start point to programs. Click administrative tools and then click computer management. Click system tools and then click device manager. Expand ide ata/atapi controllers. Double-Click the secondary ide channel for which you want to configure dma/pio settings. Click the advanced settings tab. In the transfer mode box click either pio only or dma if available.
resolved_text: Click start point to programs. Click administrative tools and then click computer management. Click system tools and then click device manager. Expand ide ata/atapi controllers. Double-Click the secondary ide channel for which the user wants to configure dma/pio settings. Click the advanced settings tab. In the transfer mode box click either pio only or dma if available.

original_text: Click start click settings and then click control panel. Double-Click phone and modem options. Click the modems tab. In the the following modems are installed list click the modem that you want to remove and then click remove. Click yes in response to the are you sure you want to remove the selected modem(s) from your system message.
resolved_text: Click start, click settings, and then click control panel. Double-click phone and modem options. Click the modems tab. In the following modems are installed list, click the modem that you want to remove and then click remove. Click yes in response to the are you sure you want to remove the selected modem(s) from your system message.

original_text: Click start point to settings and click control panel. Double-Click the administrative tools icon. Double-Click the computer management icon. Expand services and applications. Click on services. Double-Click the indexing service you wish to enable and set its startup to automatic or manual.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and click control panel. Double-Click the administrative tools icon. Double-Click the computer management icon. Expand services and applications. Click on services. Double-Click the indexing service you wish to enable and set the indexing service's startup to automatic or manual.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click system and then click user profiles. Click administrator and then click copy to. In the copy profile to box type the following line %systemroot%\profiles\default user. Click change. In the look in box click the name of the current computer. Click everyone click add and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click system and then click user profiles. Click administrator and then click copy to. In the copy profile to box type the following line %systemroot%\profiles\default user. Click change. In the look in box click the name of the current computer. Click everyone, click add, and then click ok.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click administrative tools. Double-Click computer management. Double-Click the services and applications branch to expand it and then click services. Click performance logs and alerts click action and then click start.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click administrative tools. Double-Click computer management. Double-Click the services and applications branch to expand the services and applications branch and then click services. Click performance logs and alerts, click action and then click start.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click administrative tools and then click services. Right-Click netmeeting remote desktop sharing and then click properties. In the startup type box click disabled and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click administrative tools and then click services. Right-Click netmeeting remote desktop sharing and then click properties. In the startup type box click disabled and then click ok.

original_text: Click start. Point to programs click administration tools and then click computer management. In computer management click the disk management folder under storage option you may need to click the plus sign (+) beside storage option to display all of the storage options. Click the drive c: that you want to clean up. Right-Click the drive c: and then click properties in the properties dialog box click disk cleanup.
resolved_text: Click start. Point to programs, click administration tools, and then click computer management. In computer management, click the disk management folder under the storage option; you may need to click the plus sign (+) beside the storage option to display all of the storage options. Click the drive c: that you want to clean up. Right-click the drive c: and then click properties. In the properties dialog box, click disk cleanup.

original_text: Click start and then click run. In the open box type regedit and then click ok. Locate and click the following registry key hkey_current_user \ control panel \ desktop. In the right pane double-click paintdesktopversion. Type 1 in the value data box and then click ok. Click exit on the registry menu.
resolved_text: Click start and then click run. In the open box type regedit and then click ok. Locate and click the following registry key hkey_current_user \ control panel \ desktop. In the right pane double-click paintdesktopversion. Type 1 in the value data box and then click ok. Click exit on the registry menu.

original_text: In the printers folder click server properties on the file menu. Click the ports tab click the com2 port you want to remove click delete port and then click yes.
resolved_text: In the printers folder click server properties on the file menu. Click the ports tab, click the COM2 port you want to remove, click delete port, and then click yes.

original_text: Click start and then click run. In the open box type dcomcnfg and then click ok. In the distributed com configuration properties dialog box click the default properties tab. Click to select the enable distributed com on this computer check box. Under default authentication level click connect. Under default impersonation level click identify. Click the applications tab click {000c101c-0000-0000-c000-000000000046} and then click properties. Click the general tab and then click default in the authentication level box. Click the security tab and then click use default access permissions use default launch permissions and use default configuration permissions. Click apply and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start and then click run. In the open box type dcomcnfg and then click ok. In the distributed com configuration properties dialog box click the default properties tab. Click to select the enable distributed com on this computer check box. Under default authentication level click connect. Under default impersonation level click identify. Click the applications tab click {000c101c-0000-0000-c000-000000000046} and then click properties. Click the general tab and then click default in the authentication level box. Click the security tab and then click use default access permissions, use default launch permissions, and use default configuration permissions. Click apply and then click ok.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click network and dial-up connections. Right-Click the icon for your isp and then click properties. Click the networking tab click internet protocol (tcp/ip) and then click properties. Click advanced and then click the options tab. Click ip security and then click properties. In the use this ip security policy box click client (respond only) or do not use ipsec. Click ok or close until all dialog boxes are closed.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click network and dial-up connections. Right-Click the icon for your ISP and then click properties. Click the networking tab, click internet protocol (TCP/IP), and then click properties. Click advanced and then click the options tab. Click IP security and then click properties. In the use this IP security policy box, click client (respond only) or do not use IPsec. Click OK or close until all dialog boxes are closed.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click network and dial-up connections. Right-Click the connection you want to use to connect to the isp and then click properties. Click the networking tab. Click settings. Click to clear the negotiate multi-link for single link connections check box. Click ok. Click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click network and dial-up connections. Right-Click the connection you want to use to connect to the ISP and then click properties. Click the networking tab. Click settings. Click to clear the negotiate multi-link for single link connections check box. Click OK. Click OK.

original_text: Click start point to programs point to administrative tools and then click computer management. Double-Click local users and groups. Click users and then double-click the user you want to add to the local administrator group or the power user group. Click the member of tab click add and then click either the administrators or power users group. Click add click ok and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to programs point to administrative tools and then click computer management. Double-click local users and groups. Click users and then double-click the user you want to add to the local administrator group or the power user group. Click the member of tab, click add, and then click either the administrators or power users group. Click add, click ok, and then click ok.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click printers. Double-Click add printer. Click next click local printer and then click to clear the automatically detect and install my plug and play printer check box click next. Click the appropriate port for the printer and then click next. In the manufacturers box click epson in the printers box click epson stylus color 800 esc/p 2 and then click next.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click printers. Double-Click add printer. Click next, click local printer, and then click to clear the automatically detect and install my plug and play printer check box, click next. Click the appropriate port for the printer and then click next. In the manufacturers box, click Epson; in the printers box, click Epson Stylus Color 800 ESC/P 2 and then click next.

original_text: Double-Click user configuration and then double-click administrative templates. Double-Click system and then double-click group policy. Double-Click automatic updates to .adm files and click enabled.
resolved_text: Double-Click user configuration and then double-click administrative templates. Double-Click system and then double-click group policy. Double-Click automatic updates to .adm files and click enabled.

original_text: Double-Click the root ca certificate. Click install certificate to start the certificate import wizard. Click next. Click place all certificates in the following store and then click browse. Click to select the show physical stores check box. Expand trusted root certification authorities and then select local computer. Click ok. Click next and then click finish.
resolved_text: Double-Click the root CA certificate. Click install certificate to start the certificate import wizard. Click next. Click place all certificates in the following store and then click browse. Click to select the show physical stores check box. Expand trusted root certification authorities and then select local computer. Click ok. Click next and then click finish.

original_text: Right-Click local computer policy and click properties. Click to clear the disable user configuration settings and disable computer configuration settings check boxes.
resolved_text: Right-Click local computer policy and click properties. Click to clear the disable user configuration settings and disable computer configuration settings check boxes.

original_text: In control panel open the printers or printers and faxes folder. Right-Click your postscript printer and then click properties. Click printing preferences click layout and then click advanced. Double-Click postscript options and then click truetype font download option. In the true type font download option box click automatic if automatic is already selected click outline or bitmap.
resolved_text: In control panel open the printers or printers and faxes folder. Right-Click your postscript printer and then click properties. Click printing preferences, click layout, and then click advanced. Double-Click postscript options and then click truetype font download option. In the true type font download option box click automatic; if automatic is already selected, click outline or bitmap.

original_text: Start device manager. Click view and then click show hidden devices. In the right pane of device manager click non-plug and play drivers. Double-Click afd networking support environment. Under device usage select the use this device (enable) check box and then click ok.
resolved_text: Start device manager. Click view and then click show hidden devices. In the right pane of device manager click non-plug and play drivers. Double-Click afd networking support environment. Under device usage select the use this device (enable) check box and then click ok.

original_text: Click start point to settings click control panel and then double-click internet options. On the content tab click disable in the content advisor section.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings, click control panel, and then double-click internet options. On the content tab, click disable in the content advisor section.

original_text: Click start click run in the open box type gpedit.msc and then click ok. In the group policy window expand computer configuration expand administrative templates expand system and then double-click group policy. Double-Click disk quota policy processing and then on the policy tab click to select enable. Click to clear the following two check boxes allow processing across a slow network connection do not apply during periodic background processing. Click to select the process even if the group policy objects have not changed check box and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start, click run, in the open box type gpedit.msc and then click ok. In the group policy window, expand computer configuration, expand administrative templates, expand system, and then double-click group policy. Double-click disk quota policy processing and then on the policy tab click to select enable. Click to clear the following two check boxes: allow processing across a slow network connection, do not apply during periodic background processing. Click to select the process even if the group policy objects have not changed check box and then click ok.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click folder options. Click the file types tab. Scroll down to and click the .zip extension. Click advanced. Verify that confirm open after download is selected.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. Double-Click folder options. Click the file types tab. Scroll down to and click the .zip extension. Click advanced. Verify that confirm open after download is selected.

original_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. In control panel double-click internet options. In the internet properties dialog box click the programs tab. Under internet programs in the e-mail list click eudora. Click apply and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings and then click control panel. In control panel double-click internet options. In the internet properties dialog box click the programs tab. Under internet programs in the e-mail list click Eudora. Click apply and then click OK.

original_text: Click start point to all programs point to accessories point to communications and then click remote desktop connection. Click options and on the general tab click open and locate the default.rdp file for your connection if you want to open a saved connection click open. C:10: u:12 p:-1. - left click (131596-0-l-2) [Item] Default.rdp.
resolved_text: Click start point to all programs point to accessories point to communications and then click remote desktop connection. Click options and on the general tab click open and locate the default.rdp file for your connection. If you want to open a saved connection, click open. C:10: u:12 p:-1. - left click (131596-0-l-2) [Item] Default.rdp.

original_text: Start internet explorer. On the tools menu click internet options and then click the connections tab. Click the appropriate connection and then click settings. Under automatic configuration click to clear the automatically detect settings check box. Under proxy server click to select the use a proxy server for this connection (these settings will not apply to other connections) check box. If do not want to use the proxy server for local (intranet) addresses click to select the bypass proxy server for local addresses check box. Click ok and then click ok again.
resolved_text: Start Internet Explorer. On the tools menu click Internet options and then click the connections tab. Click the appropriate connection and then click settings. Under automatic configuration click to clear the automatically detect settings check box. Under proxy server click to select the use a proxy server for this connection (the settings will not apply to other connections) check box. If you do not want to use the proxy server for local (intranet) addresses click to select the bypass proxy server for local addresses check box. Click OK and then click OK again.

original_text: Click start select settings click control panel click printers and other hardware and then click printers and faxes. Click add a printer to start the add printer wizard and then click next. Click local printer click to clear the automatically detect and install check box and then click next. In the use the following port box select the printer port to be used for example select lpt1. Click next and then click have disk. In the copy manufacturer's files from box type c:\path\printers\nt4\i386 where path is the path of your windows nt 4.0 cd. Click browse and then click the hplj6.inf file. Click open and then click ok. In the manufacturers list click hp and then in the printers list click hp laserjet 4si click next.
resolved_text: Click start, select settings, click control panel, click printers and other hardware, and then click printers and faxes. Click add a printer to start the add printer wizard and then click next. Click local printer, click to clear the automatically detect and install check box, and then click next. In the use the following port box, select the printer port to be used; for example, select lpt1. Click next and then click have disk. In the copy manufacturer's files from box, type c:\path\printers\nt4\i386 where path is the path of your Windows NT 4.0 CD. Click browse and then click the hplj6.inf file. Click open and then click ok. In the manufacturers list, click hp and then in the printers list, click hp laserjet 4si, click next.

original_text: Click start and then click run. In the open box type regedit and then click ok. In registry editor expand the following registry subkey hkey_local_machine \ system \ currentcontrolset \ control \ session manager. Click export registry file on the registry menu. In the file name box type session manager key and then click save. In the right pane of the registry editor window right-click resourcetimeoutcount and then on the shortcut menu that appears click delete. On the confirm value delete message that appears click yes. Click exit on the registry menu.
resolved_text: Click start and then click run. In the open box type regedit and then click ok. In registry editor expand the following registry subkey hkey_local_machine \ system \ currentcontrolset \ control \ session manager. Click export registry file on the registry menu. In the file name box type session manager key and then click save. In the right pane of the registry editor window right-click resourcetimeoutcount and then on the shortcut menu that appears click delete. On the confirm value delete message that appears click yes. Click exit on the registry menu.

original_text: On the computer running windows 2000 right-click my network places and then click properties. Right-Click the direct cable connection and then click properties. On the security tab click advanced (custom settings) and then click settings. Click to select the allow older ms-chap version for windows 95 servers check box click ok and then click ok.
resolved_text: On the computer running Windows 2000, right-click My Network Places and then click Properties. Right-click the Direct Cable Connection and then click Properties. On the Security tab, click Advanced (Custom Settings) and then click Settings. Click to select the Allow Older MS-CHAP Version for Windows 95 Servers check box, click OK, and then click OK.

original_text: Click start point to settings click control panel and then double-click scheduled tasks. Right-Click the task you want to disable and then click properties. On the task tab click to clear the enabled (scheduled task runs at specified time) check box and then click ok.
resolved_text: Click start point to settings, click control panel, and then double-click scheduled tasks. Right-click the task you want to disable and then click properties. On the task tab, click to clear the enabled (scheduled task runs at specified time) check box and then click ok.
